globals.name: assert
globals.summary: Raises an error if the value of its argument v is false (i.e., `nil` or `false`); otherwise, returns all its arguments. In case of error, `message` is the error object; when absent, it defaults to `"assertion failed!"`

globals.name: error
globals.summary: Terminates the last protected function called and returns message as the error object. Usually, `error` adds some information about the error position at the beginning of the message, if the message is a string.

globals.name: getmetatable
globals.summary: If object does not have a metatable, returns nil. Otherwise, if the object's metatable has a __metatable field, returns the associated value. Otherwise, returns the metatable of the given object.

globals.name: ipairs
globals.summary: Returns three values (an iterator function, the table `t`, and `0`) so that the construction ```lua for i,v in ipairs(t) do body end ``` will iterate over the key–value pairs `(1,t[1]), (2,t[2]), ...`, up to the first absent index.

globals.name: next
globals.summary: Allows a program to traverse all fields of a table. Its first argument is a table and its second argument is an index in this table. A call to `next` returns the next index of the table and its associated value. When called with `nil` as its second argument, `next` returns an initial index and its associated value. When called with the last index, or with `nil` in an empty table, `next` returns `nil`. If the second argument is absent, then it is interpreted as `nil`. In particular, you can use `next(t)` to check whether a table is empty. The order in which the indices are enumerated is not specified, *even for numeric indices*. (To traverse a table in numerical order, use a numerical `for`.) The behavior of `next` is undefined if, during the traversal, you assign any value to a non-existent field in the table. You may however modify existing fields. In particular, you may set existing fields to nil.

globals.name: pairs
globals.summary: If `t` has a metamethod `__pairs`, calls it with t as argument and returns the first three results from the call. Otherwise, returns three values: the [next](command:extension.lua.doc?["en-us/53/manual.html/pdf-next"]) function, the table `t`, and `nil`, so that the construction ```lua for k,v in pairs(t) do body end ``` will iterate over all key–value pairs of table `t`. See function [next](command:extension.lua.doc?["en-us/53/manual.html/pdf-next"]) for the caveats of modifying the table during its traversal.

globals.name: pcall
globals.summary: Calls the function `f` with the given arguments in *protected mode*. This means that any error inside `f` is not propagated; instead, `pcall` catches the error and returns a status code. Its first result is the status code (a boolean), which is true if the call succeeds without errors. In such case, `pcall` also returns all results from the call, after this first result. In case of any error, `pcall` returns `false` plus the error object.

globals.name: rawequal
globals.summary: Checks whether v1 is equal to v2, without invoking the `__eq` metamethod.

globals.name: rawget
globals.summary: Gets the real value of `table[index]`, without invoking the `__index` metamethod.

globals.name: rawlen
globals.summary: Returns the length of the object `v`, without invoking the `__len` metamethod.

globals.name: rawset
globals.summary: Sets the real value of `table[index]` to `value`, without using the `__newindex` metavalue. `table` must be a table, `index` any value different from `nil` and `NaN`, and `value` any Lua value. This function returns `table`.

globals.name: select
globals.summary: If `index` is a number, returns all arguments after argument number `index`; a negative number indexes from the end (`-1` is the last argument). Otherwise, `index` must be the string `"#"`, and `select` returns the total number of extra arguments it received.

globals.name: setmetatable
globals.summary: Sets the metatable for the given table. If `metatable` is `nil`, removes the metatable of the given table. If the original metatable has a `__metatable` field, raises an error. This function returns `table`. To change the metatable of other types from Lua code, you must use the debug library ([§6.10](command:extension.lua.doc?["en-us/53/manual.html/6.10"])).

globals.name: tonumber
globals.summary: When called with no `base`, `tonumber` tries to convert its argument to a number. If the argument is already a number or a string convertible to a number, then `tonumber` returns this number; otherwise, it returns `fail`. The conversion of strings can result in integers or floats, according to the lexical conventions of Lua (see [§3.1](command:extension.lua.doc?["en-us/53/manual.html/3.1"])). The string may have leading and trailing spaces and a sign.

globals.name: tostring
globals.summary: Receives a value of any type and converts it to a string in a human-readable format. If the metatable of `v` has a `__tostring` field, then `tostring` calls the corresponding value with `v` as argument, and uses the result of the call as its result. Otherwise, if the metatable of `v` has a `__name` field with a string value, `tostring` may use that string in its final result. For complete control of how numbers are converted, use [string.format](command:extension.lua.doc?["en-us/53/manual.html/pdf-string.format"]).

globals.name: type
globals.summary: | "nil" | "number" | "string" | "boolean" | "table" | "function" | "thread" | "userdata" Returns the type of its only argument, coded as a string. The possible results of this function are `"nil"` (a string, not the value `nil`), `"number"`, `"string"`, `"boolean"`, `"table"`, `"function"`, `"thread"`, and `"userdata"`.

globals.name: xpcall
globals.summary: Calls function `f` with the given arguments in protected mode with a new message handler.

functions.name: abs
functions.summary: Returns the absolute value of `x`.

functions.name: acos
functions.summary: Returns the arc cosine of `x` (in radians).

functions.name: asin
functions.summary: Returns the arc sine of `x` (in radians).

functions.name: atan
functions.summary: Returns the arc tangent of `y/x` (in radians).

functions.name: ceil
functions.summary: Returns the smallest integral value larger than or equal to `x`.

functions.name: cos
functions.summary: Returns the cosine of `x` (assumed to be in radians).

functions.name: deg
functions.summary: Converts the angle `x` from radians to degrees.

functions.name: exp
functions.summary: Returns the value `e^x` (where `e` is the base of natural logarithms).

functions.name: floor
functions.summary: Returns the largest integral value smaller than or equal to `x`.

functions.name: fmod
functions.summary: Returns the remainder of the division of `x` by `y` that rounds the quotient towards zero.

functions.name: log
functions.summary: Returns the logarithm of `x` in the given base.

functions.name: max
functions.summary: Returns the argument with the maximum value, according to the Lua operator `<`.

functions.name: min
functions.summary: Returns the argument with the minimum value, according to the Lua operator `<`.

functions.name: modf
functions.summary: Returns the integral part of `x` and the fractional part of `x`.

functions.name: rad
functions.summary: Converts the angle `x` from degrees to radians.

functions.name: random
functions.summary: * `math.random()`: Returns a float in the range [0,1). * `math.random(n)`: Returns a integer in the range [1, n]. * `math.random(m, n)`: Returns a integer in the range [m, n].

functions.name: randomseed
functions.summary: Sets `x` as the "seed" for the pseudo-random generator.

functions.name: sin
functions.summary: Returns the sine of `x` (assumed to be in radians).

functions.name: sqrt
functions.summary: Returns the square root of `x`.

functions.name: tan
functions.summary: Returns the tangent of `x` (assumed to be in radians).

constants.name: huge
constants.description: A value larger than any other numeric value.

functions.name: byte
functions.summary: Returns the internal numeric codes of the characters `s[i], s[i+1], ..., s[j]`.

functions.name: char
functions.summary: Returns a string with length equal to the number of arguments, in which each character has the internal numeric code equal to its corresponding argument.

functions.name: dump
functions.summary: Returns a string containing a binary representation (a *binary chunk*) of the given function.

functions.name: find
functions.summary: Looks for the first match of `pattern` (see [§6.4.1](command:extension.lua.doc?["en-us/53/manual.html/6.4.1"])) in the string.
functions.returns: integer|nil start, integer|nil end, any|nil ... captured

functions.name: format
functions.summary: Returns a formatted version of its variable number of arguments following the description given in its first argument.

functions.name: gmatch
functions.summary: Returns an iterator function. Each call to that iterator continues matching `pattern` (see [§6.4.1](command:extension.lua.doc?["en-us/53/manual.html/6.4.1"])) over s and returns all captures. The following example iterates over all words in string s, printing one per line: ```lua s = "hello world from Lua" for w in string.gmatch(s, "%a+") do print(w) end ```

functions.name: gsub
functions.summary: Returns a copy of s where all occurrences of `pattern` (or the first n occurrences if n is given) are replaced by repl (see [§6.4.1](command:extension.lua.doc?["en-us/53/manual.html/6.4.1"])).

functions.name: len
functions.summary: Returns its length.

functions.name: lower
functions.summary: Returns a copy of this string with all uppercase letters changed to lowercase.

functions.name: match
functions.summary: Looks for the first match of `pattern` (see [§6.4.1](command:extension.lua.doc?["en-us/53/manual.html/6.4.1"])) in the string. If it finds one, `match` returns the captures; otherwise it returns nil.

functions.name: rep
functions.summary: Returns a string that is the concatenation of `n` copies of the string `s` separated by the string `sep`.

functions.name: reverse
functions.summary: Returns a string that is the string `s` reversed.

functions.name: sub
functions.summary: Returns the substring of the string that starts at `i` and continues until `j`.

functions.name: upper
functions.summary: Returns a copy of this string with all lowercase letters changed to uppercase.

functions.name: concat
functions.summary: Given a list where all elements are strings or numbers, returns the string `list[i]..sep..list[i+1] ··· sep..list[j]`.

functions.name: insert
functions.summary: Inserts element `value` at position `pos` in `list`.

functions.name: pack
functions.summary: Returns a new table with all arguments stored into keys `1`, `2`, etc. and with a field `"n"` with the total number of arguments.

functions.name: remove
functions.summary: Removes from `list` the element at position `pos`, returning the value of the removed element.

functions.name: sort
functions.summary: Sorts list elements in a given order, *in-place*, from `list[1]` to `list[#list]`.

functions.name: unpack
functions.summary: Returns the elements from the given list. This function is equivalent to ```lua return list[i], list[i+1], ···, list[j] ``` By default, `i` is `1` and `j` is `#list`. [1]?: T1, [2]?: T2, [3]?: T3, [4]?: T4, [5]?: T5, [6]?: T6, [7]?: T7, [8]?: T8, [9]?: T9, [10]?: T10, }

functions.name: create
functions.summary: Creates a new coroutine, with body `f`. `f` must be a function. Returns this new coroutine, an object with type `"thread"`.

functions.name: isyieldable
functions.summary: Returns true when the running coroutine can yield.

functions.name: resume
functions.summary: Starts or continues the execution of coroutine `co`.

functions.name: running
functions.summary: Returns the running coroutine plus a boolean, true when the running coroutine is the main one.

functions.name: status
functions.summary: Returns the status of coroutine `co`. | '"running"'   # Is running. | '"suspended"' # Is suspended or not started. | '"normal"'    # Is active but not running. | '"dead"'      # Has finished or stopped with an error.

functions.name: wrap
functions.summary: Creates a new coroutine, with body `f`; `f` must be a function. Returns a function that resumes the coroutine each time it is called.

functions.name: yield
functions.summary: Suspends the execution of the calling coroutine.

functions.name: char
functions.summary: Receives zero or more integers, converts each one to its corresponding UTF-8 byte sequence and returns a string with the concatenation of all these sequences.

functions.name: codepoint
functions.summary: Returns the codepoints (as integers) from all characters in `s` that start between byte position `i` and `j` (both included).

functions.name: codes
functions.summary: Returns values so that the construction ```lua for p, c in utf8.codes(s) do body end ``` will iterate over all UTF-8 characters in string s, with p being the position (in bytes) and c the code point of each character. It raises an error if it meets any invalid byte sequence.

functions.name: len
functions.summary: Returns the number of UTF-8 characters in string `s` that start between positions `i` and `j` (both inclusive).

functions.name: offset
functions.summary: Returns the position (in bytes) where the encoding of the `n`-th character of `s` (counting from position `i`) starts.
